How many countries are in Africa? Answer with a number only.
note: 54 UN-recognised sovereign states in Africa (Worldometer, Wikipedia)

prompt: How many bones does an adult human have? Answer with a number only.
note: Standard adult human skeleton: 206 bones (Cleveland Clinic, Wikipedia)

prompt: How many teeth does a fully-dentitioned adult human have? Answer with a number only.
note: 32 permanent teeth including wisdom teeth (Cleveland Clinic, Healthline)

prompt: How many chambers does the human heart have? Answer with a number only.
note: 4 chambers: right atrium, left atrium, right ventricle, left ventricle (Cleveland Clinic)

prompt: How many stripes are on the United States flag? Answer with a number only.
note: 13 alternating red and white stripes representing the original 13 colonies (USA.gov, Wikipedia)

prompt: How many keys are on a standard full-size piano? Answer with a number only.
note: 88 keys (52 white, 36 black) on a standard piano (Aulart, Markson Pianos)

prompt: How many fluid ounces are in one US gallon? Answer with a number only.
note: 1 US gallon = 128 US fluid ounces (YETI, Inch Calculator)

prompt: How many planets are in our solar system? Answer with a number only.
note: 8 planets per IAU 2006 definition: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune (NASA)

prompt: How many sides does a standard stop sign have? Answer with a number only.
note: Stop signs are octagons — 8 sides (Wikipedia, Mental Floss)

prompt: How many holes are on a standard golf course? Answer with a number only.
note: 18 holes is the universal standard for a regulation golf course (Wikipedia, Northbridge Golf Club)

prompt: How many elements are on the periodic table? Answer with a number only.
note: 118 confirmed elements on the modern periodic table (Wikipedia, BYJU'S)

prompt: In what year was the Eiffel Tower completed? Answer with a number only.
note: Construction completed March 31, 1889 (Official Eiffel Tower website, Wikipedia)

prompt: How many valves does the human heart have? Answer with a number only.
note: 4 valves: aortic, mitral, pulmonary, tricuspid (Cleveland Clinic, OHSU)

prompt: How many moons does Mars have? Answer with a number only.
note: Mars has 2 moons: Phobos and Deimos (NASA Science)

prompt: How many stars appear on the flag of China? Answer with a number only.
note: Five golden stars: one large and four smaller (Wikipedia, Koryo Tours)

prompt: How many official time zones does China use? Answer with a number only.
note: China uses a single time zone (UTC+8) despite spanning 5 geographical zones (Wikipedia, TimeAndDate)

prompt: How many players from each team are on the court at one time in a standard basketball game? Answer with a number only.
note: 5 players per team on court at a time; universal rule (FIBA, NBA, NCAA)

prompt: How many stars are on the flag of the United States? Answer with a number only.
note: 50 white stars representing the 50 states (USA.gov, Wikipedia)

prompt: How many sides does a pentagon have? Answer with a number only.
note: A pentagon has 5 sides by definition (Wikipedia, PrepScholar)

prompt: How many sides does a hexagon have? Answer with a number only.
note: A hexagon has 6 sides by definition (Wikipedia, DreamBox)

prompt: How many US presidents have been assassinated while in office? Answer with a number only.
note: Lincoln (1865), Garfield (1881), McKinley (1901), Kennedy (1963). Models may confabulate 2 or 3. (Wikipedia, Booth Western Art Museum)

prompt: What is the only country whose name starts with the letter O? Answer in one word, lowercase.
note: Oman is the only sovereign country whose English name begins with O. Models often guess 'none' or incorrectly cite Austria. (Worldometer, CountryReports Blog)

prompt: How many official time zones does China use? Answer with a number only.
note: China uses a single official time zone (UTC+8) despite spanning 5 geographical zones. Models frequently answer 5. (Wikipedia, TimeAndDate)

prompt: In the sequence of planets from the Sun, what number planet is Earth? Answer with a digit only.
note: Mercury=1, Venus=2, Earth=3. Straightforward but models sometimes insert incorrect orderings. (NASA)

prompt: How many sides does an octagon have? Answer with a digit only.
note: An octagon has exactly 8 sides. Included because models occasionally confuse octagon with hexagon under pressure. (Wikipedia)

prompt: How many Great Lakes are there in North America? Answer with a number only.
note: The five Great Lakes are Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, Ontario. Models sometimes answer 4 or 6. (Wikipedia)

prompt: What is the number of the Amendment to the US Constitution that abolished slavery? Answer with a number only.
note: The 13th Amendment (1865) abolished slavery. Models may confuse with the 14th (citizenship) or 15th (voting). (Wikipedia)

prompt: How many continents are there on Earth? Answer with a number only.
note: Standard seven-continent model: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia/Oceania, Europe, North America, South America. Models may answer 5 or 6 depending on alternate models. The prompt relies on the dominant seven-continent model taught in English-language education. (Wikipedia)

prompt: How many faces does a cube have? Answer with a digit only.
note: A cube has exactly 6 faces. Adversarial because some models conflate faces/edges/vertices under stress. (Wikipedia)

prompt: In the standard musical scale, how many notes are in one octave (including both the starting and ending note)? Answer with a number only.
note: Do-Re-Mi-Fa-Sol-La-Ti-Do = 8 notes when both endpoints are counted (the word 'octave' derives from Latin for 8). Models often answer 7 (the distinct pitches) without counting the repeated tonic. (Wikipedia)
